diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Key feature distinguishing cholecystitis from self-limiting biliary colic.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known gallstones with previous shorter-lived episodes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Establishes the underlying gallstone disease predisposing to cholecystitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain worse on deep breathing, radiating to right shoulder blade

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Consistent with inflammation irritating the diaphragm and referred shoulder-tip pain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive Murphy's sign and mild scleral icterus

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Classic examination finding; clinician-only finding sealed from telehealth chat.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Pain lasting beyond 6 hours with fever and shoulder-tip radiation, in a patient with known gallstones, together confirm progression to acute cholecystitis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent abdominal ultrasound and blood tests (FBC, CRP, LFTs) in the Emergency Department
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms gallbladder wall thickening/pericholecystic fluid and inflammatory markers.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: IV antibiotics and surgical review for likely cholecystectomy
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Standard treatment for confirmed acute cholecystitis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Oral analgesia and routine GP follow-up in a few days
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Would dangerously delay treatment and risk gallbladder perforation.
